description: Swords, axes, bows, and instruments of war — from common steel to exotic designs.
items: Adamantine Knuckle
An indestructible fist-plate shattering stone with each punch, offering zero reach and grinding the joints.
Alchemist's Fire
A sticky incendiary flask erupting into clinging flames on impact, nearly impossible to extinguish and risky to carry.
Arcane Orb
A hovering glass globe firing energy bolts hands-free, prone to shattering under disruption magic.
Arquebus
A matchlock long gun that changed siege warfare forever, requiring a rest hook and dry weather to fire.
Arrow
A straight ash shaft tipped with iron and fletched with goose feather, fragile on impact and quickly spent.
Arrowhead
A sharpened iron broadhead socketed for a shaft, small enough to conceal in a closed fist.
Atlatl
A spear-throwing lever multiplying javelin velocity for hunting large game, clumsy in close combat.
Ballista
A torsion-powered siege engine hurling iron darts through fortress walls, stationary and crew-dependent.
Bardiche
A polearm crowned with a crescent blade for chopping at cavalry legs, its heavy head tiring to swing.
Bat
A thick wooden bludgeon tapered at the grip, blunt and splintering but punishing at full swing.
Battering Ram
An iron-capped log swung by a team to splinter gates, needing overhead cover against arrows and oil.
Battleaxe
A broad-headed axe that bites deep into shields, slower to recover than any sword.
Bearded Axe
An axe with an extended lower blade that hooks shields and limbs, its thin beard weak against plate.
Bident
A twin-pronged fork built to pin shields and creatures in place, limited in lethal thrusting depth.
Billhook
An agrarian polearm sporting hook and spike for clearing pike blocks, clumsy in a direct thrust.
Blade Staff
A double-ended polearm with a blade at each tip, devastating in a spin but perilous in tight quarters.
Bladed Tonfa
A tonfa fitted with a forearm blade that blocks and slices in one rotation, risking self-injury on quick spins.
Blood Spear
An accursed barbed spear that drinks life from its victims to heal the wielder, corrupting with every feed.
Bloodletter Saw
A jagged serrated blade inflicting grievous bleeding wounds, its teeth catching on bone and slowing follow-up cuts.
Blowgun
A slender tube delivering poisoned darts in perfect silence, worthless against even leather armor.
Blowpipe Dart Frog
A jungle reed pipe firing venom-tipped darts in silence, its aim spoiled by any breeze.
Blunderbuss
A trumpet-mouthed gun spraying shot in a wide cone, devastating at close range and useless beyond it.
Bo Staff
A flexible Eastern staff enabling rapid spins and sweeps, lacking the mass to crack armor.
Boiling Oil (Cauldron)
A defensive cauldron pouring scalding liquid onto climbers below, limited in supply and perilous to handle.
Bolo Gun
A spring-loaded launcher hurling weighted cords to tangle legs and wings, useless once its single shot is spent.
Bolt
A stubby crossbow quarrel with stiff vanes and a squared iron tip, punching hard over short distance.
Boomerang
A curved throwing stick that returns on a miss — clever but limited in genuine lethality.
Booby Trap
A concealed mechanism of trip wire and sharpened stakes, deadly to the unwary but easy to disarm once spotted.
Broadsword
A wide medieval blade splitting the difference between cut and thrust, mediocre at both.
Buckler
A fist-sized metal disc for deflecting blows and punching faces, sacrificing coverage for nimble parries.
Catapult
A tension-armed siege engine flinging boulders over battlements, slow to reload and wildly inaccurate.
Celestial Mace
A star-metal flanged head radiating holy light that smites fiends on contact, brittle against adamantine.
Cestus
A metal-studded fighting glove adding bone-breaking force to bare fists, offering zero reach in return.
Chain Whip
Segmented steel links wrapping limbs and deflecting blades, dangerously easy to mis-time against the wielder.
Chakram
A razor-edged Indian ring sailing far and slicing on return, bouncing unpredictably in wooded terrain.
Chaos Dagger
A shimmering blade that shifts length and edge unpredictably, as likely to cut the wielder as the target.
Claymore
A broad Scottish greatsword with flared quillons, devastating in wide sweeps but hopeless in tight corridors.
Clockwork Blade
A gear-driven segmented sword that telescopes into a whip for surprise reach, its mechanism jamming in sand.
Club
A rough length of hardwood heavy at one end, the simplest and oldest weapon known to any hand.
Cobalt Repeater
A gnomish crank-rifle spitting darts in rapid bursts, chronically prone to jams and lacking stopping power.
Composite Bow
A horn-and-sinew recurve packing tremendous power into a saddle-friendly frame, warped easily by moisture.
Crossbow
A stock-mounted prod easy for novices to shoot with lethal force, painfully slow between bolts.
Crystal Wand
A delicate crystalline rod channeling spells with pinpoint precision, shattering if struck and dead without mana.
Cudgel
A short, knotted stick weighted with lead in the head, simple and brutal in close quarters.
Cutlass
A short naval sabre with a basket hilt, built for hacking rigging and brawling on slippery decks.
Dagger
A compact blade small enough to hide in a boot, deadly up close but worthless at arm's length.
Dane Axe
A long-hafted Viking axe reaching over shield walls, demanding space and steady hands to control.
Dart
A weighted throwing spike precise enough for poison delivery, effective only at very short range.
Dirk
A long double-edged dagger suited for sudden lunges and offhand parries, lacking mass for heavy blows.
Dragonlance
A silver-inlaid lance resonating through dragon scales on impact, far too heavy for use against human foes.
Drill Spear
A clockwork spear tip spinning to bore through tough carapace, its fine gears clogging in mud and grit.
Echo Boomerang
An enchanted returning disc that tracks sound to find its target, rendered useless inside silence spells.
Energy Glaive
A crystalline polearm projecting a laser cutting edge through steel, its bright energy lines betraying every position.
Etheric Pistol
A crystal-capacitor gun phasing bullets through solid walls, completely harmless to constructs and golems.
Exploding Kunai
A thrown knife fitted with a timed fuse for devastating area damage, lethal to the thrower if the timer fails.
Falchion
Heavy and wide-bellied like a cleaver, delivering brutal chops that leave the wielder open on recovery.
Felling Axe
A long-handled woodsman's axe ground thin for biting deep into timber, dangerously slow to swing in a fight.
Flail
A chain-linked ball whipping around shields with terrifying force, dangerously hard to control mid-swing.
Flash Powder
A bright alchemical burst that blinds every nearby eye, nonlethal and ruined by moisture.
Flintlock Pistol
An early single-shot firearm delivering shocking impact and deafening report, then a long useless reload.
Frost Grenade
An alchemical sphere bursting into freezing shards that immobilize groups, just as dangerous to nearby allies.
Frostbrand
An ice-rimed blade trailing frozen mist that extinguishes flame, half as potent under a desert sun.
Gladiator Net
A reinforced arena net weighted for capture and blade-resistant, ineffective against anything larger than an ogre.
Glaive
A single-edged pole blade sweeping through infantry lines, inferior to spears for direct thrusting.
Golem Fist
A titanic stone gauntlet delivering seismic punches, so cumbersome it ruins spellcasting gestures entirely.
Grappling Gun
A steam-powered hook launcher granting vertical mobility in cities, bulky and nearly useless as a weapon.
Graviton Hammer
A rune-heavy hammer generating a crushing gravity well on impact, requiring a long cooldown between strikes.
Great Axe
A broad-bladed two-hander built for cleaving through shield and bone, exhausting to wield through a long fight.
Greataxe
A two-handed crescent of iron delivering devastating overhead chops, leaving the wielder exposed on a miss.
Greatbow
A massive war bow outranging even longbows, utterly impractical on horseback or in dense forest.
Greatclub
A crude wooden cudgel massive enough to crush bones through mail, easily read and parried by skilled fighters.
Greatsword
A towering two-handed blade that cleaves through ranks, exhausting to swing and slow to recover.
Grenade
A hollow iron sphere packed with black powder and fitted with a short fuse, devastating but wildly inaccurate.
Guandao
A broad-bladed Chinese polearm relying on momentum-driven chops, cumbersome for smaller warriors.
Guisarme
A long-hafted hook designed to drag riders from their saddles, weaker in cutting than a halberd.
Halberg
A tall polearm combining axe, spike, and hook on a single haft, versatile but unwieldy in close quarters.
Halberd
An axe-spike-hook trinity on a long shaft offering versatile reach, useless once fighting closes in.
Hammer
A short-hafted war hammer with a flat striking face, punishing against plate but lacking any cutting edge.
Hand Axe
A single-handed hatchet balanced for both chopping and throwing, limited in reach against longer arms.
Hand Cannon
A crude black-powder tube hitting like a small siege engine, dangerously slow to ignite and brutal in recoil.
Hand Crossbow
A one-handed crossbow concealable under a cloak, trading power and range for stealth and surprise.
Handaxe
A light single-hand axe doubling as camp tool and throwing weapon, its short haft weak against armor.
Hatchet
A compact chopping blade on a short haft, handy for splitting kindling or splitting skulls at arm's length.
Heavy Crossbow
A windlass-drawn siege crossbow punching bolts through plate, its reload measured in long, vulnerable seconds.
Hidden Blade
A spring-loaded knife concealed in a wrist sheath, deploying silently for a single lethal strike.
Hidden Crossbow
A miniature crossbow disguised inside a sleeve or shield, firing one surprise bolt before requiring a slow reload.
Hook Sword
A Chinese blade with a hooked guard for tripping and linking paired weapons, its hook weakening direct thrusts.
Iron Fan
A war fan of sharpened steel ribs deflecting darts while slashing arteries unseen, useless against plate.
Javelin
A light throwing spear ideal for skirmish harassment, lacking the mass to breach a shield wall.
Jet Pike
A rune-etched pike with rear propulsion for explosive lunging charges, limited in fuel and impossible to stop mid-thrust.
Kanabo
A spiked iron-studded club that shatters armor and morale alike, brutally heavy for ordinary mortals.
Katar
A punch dagger driving powerful straight stabs through mail, its rigid grip sacrificing reach and flexibility.
Katana
A gently curved blade renowned for razor draw-cuts, its brittle edge prone to chipping on hard armor.
Knuckles
Ridged brass rings slipped over the fingers, adding crushing weight to a bare-knuckle punch.
Kris
A wavy-bladed Indonesian dagger inflicting ragged wounds, its undulations making precise thrusts difficult.
Kukri
A forward-curved Nepalese chopper hitting like a hatchet, awkward for any style of fencing.
Kusarigama
A sickle trailing a weighted chain for entangling before the cut, nightmarishly hard to master in crowds.
Lance
A mounted spear built for devastating charge impacts, impossibly heavy and clumsy on foot.
Light Staff
A slender wooden pole tipped with a caged crystal, channeling light into searing beams but fragile under heavy blows.
Lightning Rod
A tall iron pole channeling storm bolts into clustered enemies, dead weight without weather or magic to draw from.
Living Whip
A vine-like tendril that grapples independently and regrows when severed, withering fast in fire or frost.
Long Bow
A tall stave of springy yew launching arrows at great range, demanding trained strength to draw fully.
Long Sword
A cruciform blade of tempered steel balanced for one or two hands, the quintessential knight's sidearm.
Longbow
A tall yew bow driving arrows through mail at great distance, demanding years of strength to master.
Longspear
An extended infantry spear reaching well beyond sword range, nearly impossible to maneuver indoors.
Longsword
A straight double-edged blade balanced for cut and thrust, versatile but blunted against heavy plate.
Mace
A flanged steel head focusing every ounce of force into denting armor, outreached by any polearm.
Mancatcher
A pole tipped with spring-loaded prongs for capturing targets alive, nearly useless for killing.
Maul
A two-handed sledge that cracks bones, doors, and morale — slow, telegraphed, and merciless.
Meteor Hammer
Twin iron weights on long chains delivering crushing momentum strikes, predictable once an opponent reads the rhythm.
Mining Charge
A packed bundle of black powder and slow-match fuse, meant for blasting rock but lethal in confined spaces.
Mirror Shield
A polished silver shield reflecting hostile spells back at the caster, fragile under brute physical strikes.
Moon Sickle
A silver feyblade glowing softly to heal allies while wounding foes, its light spoiling ambushes and dulled by iron.
Morning Star
A spiked ball atop a haft inflicting puncture and blunt trauma together, its heavy head ruining quick parries.
Musket
A smoothbore long gun driving armor-ignoring balls at distance, hopelessly inaccurate without massed volleys.
Naginata
A graceful Japanese polearm balanced for sweeping arcs and nimble footwork, weak against heavy shields.
Net
A weighted mesh flung to entangle foes for a killing follow-up, useless against flyers or giants.
Ninja Star
A flat steel throwing blade with sharpened points, spinning silently through the air but easily deflected by armor.
Nodachi
An oversized field sword meant for sweeping cavalry from saddles, far too long for indoor use.
Nunchaku
Paired hardwood sticks on a short chain, rapid in strike and block but desperately short in reach.
Partisan
A broad-bladed spear with side lugs for catching weapons, those same lugs snagging on withdrawal.
Phantom Chakram
An ethereal disc phasing through solid matter to strike living flesh, powerless against constructs and barriers.
Phoenix Talon
A curved dagger erupting into flame the instant it clears the sheath, scorching its scabbard with every draw.
Pike
An extraordinarily long infantry spear forming an impenetrable hedge, useless once the line breaks.
Plasma Glaive
A magitech polearm wreathed in crackling plasma arcs, melting steel on contact but draining its power cell fast.
Poison Needle Ring
A concealed ring delivering toxin through a hidden prick, holding only a single treacherous dose.
Poleaxe
A knight-killing hammer-and-beak combination built to shatter plate, punishingly heavy in prolonged combat.
Push Dagger
A palm-clenched spike built for grappling kills, offering no crossguard to protect the fingers.
Quarter Staff
A six-foot pole of seasoned oak wielded in wide arcs, forgiving to learn but feeble against heavy armor.
Quarterstaff
Six feet of seasoned hardwood offering reach, defense, and mercy — struggling against plate and sharp edges.
Ram
A heavy iron-headed timber swung by a crew to batter down doors and barricades, slow and exposed.
Rapier
A slim thrusting needle superb for duels — fast and precise but feeble against thick hides.
Recurve Bow
Curved limbs storing extra energy for snappier shots, harder to string and finicky to maintain.
Repeating Crossbow
A magazine-fed mechanism firing bolts in rapid succession, each shot weaker than a standard crossbow's.
Runebound Chain
An enchanted chain whose binding glyphs tighten on spoken command, passing uselessly through incorporeal targets.
Runic Staff
A tall staff carved with glowing rune circuits extending spell range, mundanely just a stick if the circuit breaks.
Sabre
A cavalry blade curved for galloping slashes, its thin tip lacking the punch to pierce mail.
Sai
A three-pronged iron baton designed to trap and disarm sword blades, offering almost no cutting edge.
Sandskimmer Blade
A curved sword enchanted to glide through sand without drag, utterly worthless on stone or hard ground.
Sap
A lead-filled leather cosh for dropping targets quietly, bouncing harmlessly off any decent helmet.
Scimitar
A crescent-curved edge built for mounted slashing at speed, poor at punching through heavy plate.
Scythe
A long curved blade on a wooden haft, terrifying in a reaping sweep but slow and clumsy to recover.
Serpent Blade
A segmented sword that extends like a striking snake for sudden reach, its joints weakening every parry.
Shadow Rapier
A needle of condensed darkness piercing life force through armor, dissipating entirely in bright sunlight.
Shield Bash
The brutal art of striking with a shield's edge to stun and shove, weaker than a proper weapon blow.
Short Bow
A compact stave bow quick to draw from horseback or behind cover, lacking the punch of taller bows.
Short Sword
A one-handed blade of modest length ideal for close fighting, outreached by any full-sized sword.
Shortbow
A compact stave bow firing quickly from cover or horseback, lacking the range of taller bows.
Shortsword
A light thrusting blade quick enough for alley fights, though its short reach invites longer weapons inside.
Shuriken
A small steel star meant more to distract and poison than kill, dealing negligible damage alone.
Sickle
A curved hand-blade that hooks tendons at grappling distance, too short for any honest duel.
Siege Crossbow (Arbalest)
A giant steel crossbow winning fortification duels with massive bolts, immobilizing its crew during the long crank.
Siege Tower
A wheeled wooden fortress letting troops scale walls under shelter, achingly vulnerable to fire and moats.
Sling
A leather pouch hurling stones silently over surprising distance, demanding real skill and weak against armor.
Smoke Bomb
A glass orb releasing thick choking clouds for cover and retreat, scattered uselessly by strong wind.
Sonic Spear
A high-frequency vibrating tip that slices stone without sound, its tremor betraying the wielder to keen-eared foes.
Soul Reaver Scythe
A dark-iron scythe harvesting souls to restore the wielder's vigor, terrifying allies and offering no defense.
Spear
A simple pole-and-point requiring little training in formation, outreached by pikes and outmatched alone.
Spectral Net
Ghost-silk mesh woven to entangle ethereal creatures, passing harmlessly through anything corporeal.
Spetum
A forked spear designed to trap blades between its prongs, sacrificing penetrating depth for control.
Spiked Gauntlet
An armored glove bristling with spikes that turn every grapple lethal, crippling the hand's dexterity.
Spiked Hammer
A war hammer studded with iron points that punch through plate on every swing, prone to snagging on withdrawal.
Spiked Shield
A reinforced shield with a central boss spike, punishing on the bash but prone to snagging.
Spikes
Sharpened iron caltrops scattered on the ground, crippling any foot or hoof that finds them in the dark.
Spore Bomb
A glass jar of fungal spores that blinds eyes and chokes lungs, scattered unpredictably by any wind.
Staff
A tall pole of seasoned hardwood worn smooth at the grip, humble yet effective in trained hands.
Staff Sling
A pole-mounted sling lobbing heavier shot for siege skirmishing, far slower to reload than any bow.
Stake
A sharpened length of ash or hawthorn, crude but feared by the undead and useful for impromptu defense.
Starforged Lance
A meteor-iron lance piercing magical wards around sky beasts, its heavy magnetic pull warping every nearby compass.
Steam Rifle
A boiler-powered rifle launching metal slugs on pressurized steam, needing water and warmup time to fire.
Stiletto
A needlelike triangular spike designed to punch through armor gaps, virtually useless for slashing.
Storm Bow
An elven longbow transmuting arrows into lightning bolts midflight, rendered inert in anti-magic zones.
Storm Trident
A sea-god's weapon calling lightning down on impact, its conductivity threatening the wielder in water.
Sun Hammer
A mithril warhammer storing sunlight for a blinding radiant flash, dim and drained without daylight to recharge.
Sunblade
A holy sword radiating searing light that burns undead on contact, its glow betraying every attempt at stealth.
Sword
A straight blade of forged steel with a leather-wrapped grip, the universal sidearm of soldiers and adventurers.
Tanto
A stout Japanese knife ground for piercing armor joints, dangerously short-ranged in open melee.
Tetsubo
An iron-shod staff capable of smashing gates and shields, agonizingly slow between swings.
Throwing Axe
A balanced hatchet hurled to split shields at range, leaving the thrower empty-handed afterward.
Throwing Knife
A slim balanced blade flung for silent kills, easily deflected and poor at penetrating armor.
Tonfa
A side-handled baton spinning between block and strike in one motion, ineffective against any real armor.
Tower Shield
A wall-sized wooden shield creating mobile cover for an entire rank, exhaustingly heavy to carry.
Trebuchet
A counterweight sling capable of pulverizing castle walls with ninety-kilogram stones, demanding hours to assemble.
Trident
A three-pronged fishing spear adept at pinning and disarming, its spread tines weak against armor.
Trident & Net
The gladiator's signature combo — tangling prey then skewering it, requiring rare coordination and open ground.
Trip Wire
A taut strand of thin wire strung ankle-high across a path, invisible in dim light and ruthlessly effective.
Truncheon
A short hardwood baton favored by city guards for crowd work, feeble against anything tougher than a skull.
Two Handed Axe
A massive double-bitted axe demanding both hands and a wide stance, devastating but agonizingly slow.
Two-bladed Sword
A double-ended blade with a grip at its center, flashy and intimidating but impractical for conventional swordplay.
Urumi
A flexible sword-ribbon creating deadly whirling arcs that bypass shields, lethally dangerous to wield indoors.
Voidstar Flail
A morning star containing a miniature black hole that drags targets inward, wildly unpredictable near magnetic fields.
Wakizashi
Companion blade to the katana, handy in tight quarters but outreached in any stand-up fight.
War Hammer
A heavy-headed hammer designed to cave in plate armor, its momentum carrying through blocks but slow to redirect.
War Pick
A spiked beak concentrating all force into a single point, easily glancing off moving targets.
War Scythe
A farm scythe reforged with its blade set upright for sweeping cuts, crude and badly unbalanced.
Warhammer
A hammer-and-pick head built to punch plate and hook riders, its tiny striking face easy to miss with.
Whaling Harpoon
A barbed iron spear launched from the deck to tether sea monsters, its rope dragging the wielder if the beast dives.
Whip
A braided leather lash reaching far enough to disarm and trip, dealing pitiful damage to armored foes.
Whisper Bow
A recurve enchanted to silence every arrow's flight for assassin work, weaker in draw than true war bows.
Zweihander
Massive Germanic steel long enough to shatter pike formations, demanding enormous strength to wield at all.
